title: PRISM gates.json schema
description: Structural shape for gates.json — the per-persona gate configuration that run-gates.mjs, ownership-guard.mjs, and evidence-ledger.mjs read at runtime. This file is the schema; the populated data lives at .claude/hooks/gates.json, authored in Phase 1 (clove entry) and Phase 5 (full roster rollout). No per-persona data lives here — only the shape every entry must conform to. See .prism/references/enforcement/report-contract.md for the report contract this data enforces, and .prism/architect/_toolkit/enforcement-floor.md for the class taxonomy that determines which gate fields are required per persona.

PersonaGateEntry.description: Gate configuration for one persona. The key is the persona's gates.json identifier (lowercase, matching the 'persona' field in report.json — e.g. 'clove', 'briar', 'winston'). Every persona in the roster that carries a verdict gets an entry; utilities (prism-handoff, prism-review-loop, prism-skill-forge) do not.

writes_report_to.description: Path where this persona writes its report.json, relative to the worktree root. Always .prism/evidence/<runKey>/report.json at runtime — the runKey token is substituted by the hook from agent_id ?? session_id. Record the pattern, not a resolved path.

preconditions.description: Checks that must pass before the persona's evidence gates run. A failed precondition short-circuits to needs-replan or blocked rather than failing the gate — it means the persona couldn't have done its job, not that it did its job badly. Class C personas have an empty array here; Class B and Class A personas carry structural preconditions (e.g. 'plan file exists', 'report.json written').

gates.description: Evidence gates the runtime runs to ratify or override the persona's claimed verdict. Class A personas carry hard evidence gates (exit-code checks); Class B personas carry structural/coherence gates (file-presence, report-shape); Class C personas have an empty array (floor is ownership + contract only). See enforcement-floor.md for the class taxonomy.

unverifiable_boxes.description: Named claims the runtime cannot check — quality judgments, design decisions, content correctness. Listing them here is the explicit acknowledgment that these stay structurally trusted. The Briar caveat (ADR-0067) is the canonical example: review quality cannot be gated, only review act and report coherence can. This array prevents a future Phase from chasing a gate that cannot exist.
examples: review quality — whether Briar's findings are correct, not just that findings exist
design soundness — whether Winston's plan is good, not just that tasks are present

allowed_routes.description: The set of valid next_route values for this persona. Derived from the persona's ## Next persona section in its skill file; populated by Phase 5. The gate's coherence check reads this array — a next_route value not in this set is rejected. Always includes 'winston' for any persona that can emit needs-replan, and 'human' for any persona that can emit blocked or needs-human.

Precondition.description: A structural check that must pass before evidence gates run. Unlike a gate, a failed precondition signals the run environment was wrong (plan absent, report not written) rather than that the persona's work failed.

id.description: Short slug identifying this precondition (e.g. 'plan-exists', 'report-written'). Matches the key in report.json's checklist when applicable.

description.description: Human-readable label for the precondition (e.g. 'Branch plan file exists', 'report.json written before stop').

on_fail.description: Verdict to inject if this precondition fails. needs-replan when the environment problem traces to a plan gap; blocked when it traces to a dependency or environment failure; needs-human when human input is required to unblock.

Gate.description: An evidence gate the runtime runs to verify a persona's claimed checklist item. A gate corresponds to one checklist key in report.json. If the gate's check fails and the persona claimed that checklist item as true, the gate overrides the verdict.

id.description: Short slug for this gate (e.g. 'types', 'tests', 'lint', 'changelog-file-written'). Must match the key used in report.json's checklist object.

description.description: Human-readable label (e.g. 'TypeScript types pass', 'Test suite passes', 'Changelog file written to output path').

checklist_key.description: The key in report.json's checklist this gate verifies. Must match the gate's id — kept as an explicit field so the mapping is machine-readable without parsing the id.

source.description: How the gate obtains its evidence. 'fresh' — runs the check command now at Stop time. 'ledger' — reads the evidence ledger (.prism/evidence/<runKey>/ledger.jsonl) for the most recent matching command exit code, falling back to 'fresh' if the ledger has no matching entry. Use 'ledger' for expensive commands (test suites) that the persona already ran during its session; use 'fresh' for fast checks (tsc, file-presence) where re-running at Stop is cheap. Resolved in Phase 3 after the tokenization seam is in place.

on_fail.description: Verdict override to inject when the check fails and the persona claimed this item as true. 'done-override' — the persona claimed done; override to needs-fix (the most common case for Class A implementer gates). 'needs-fix' — the gate explicitly routes to the implementer. 'blocked' — environment failure, not implementation failure. 'needs-replan' — a structural problem the plan must address.

CheckSpec.description: The actual check the runtime runs for a gate or precondition. Two kinds: command checks (run a command and inspect exit code) and file checks (assert a path exists and optionally validate its contents). The kind field determines which other fields apply.

kind.description: 'command' — run a shell command and check its exit code. 'file-exists' — assert a file or directory exists at the given path. 'file-validates' — assert a file exists and parses/validates (e.g. JSON parse, schema validation).

command.description: Required when kind is 'command'. The command to run, expressed as a token reference (e.g. '{{commands.typecheck}}') or a literal string. Token references are resolved at runtime from config.json's commands map (Phase 3). Literal strings are acceptable in Phase 1 before the tokenization seam exists; Phase 3 converts them to tokens.

path.description: Required when kind is 'file-exists' or 'file-validates'. Path to check, relative to the worktree root. May contain the ${runKey} token, substituted at runtime.

schema.description: Optional when kind is 'file-validates'. Path to a JSON Schema file to validate the target against, relative to the repo root. When absent, 'file-validates' only checks that the file parses as valid JSON.

exit_code.description: Optional when kind is 'command'. Expected exit code; defaults to 0. Use when a non-zero exit is the success condition.

OwnershipMatrix.description: Declares what this persona may and may not do. Enforced before the action by ownership-guard.mjs (PreToolUse). The matrix is the negative half of handoff enforcement — it makes doing the next lane's work impossible, so handoff is the only remaining move. See enforcement-floor.md for the ownership principle.

may_write.description: Glob patterns of file paths this persona is allowed to write. The ownership guard matches the target path of every Edit, Write, and MultiEdit tool call against these globs before allowing the write. A path that matches none of the globs is denied with a message naming the allowed patterns. Phase 5 verifies these globs against what the skill body genuinely writes — may_write must match actual write behavior, not aspirational scope.

may_not_run.description: Command substrings this persona may never run as a Bash command. The ownership guard substring-matches every Bash tool call against these values before allowing the command. A match is denied before execution. Encodes real boundaries: merge commands for implementers (Clove can't merge — must hand to human); merge commands for every reviewer (Eric never merges — ADR-0011); all merge and code-write commands for Sol.
